strengths: Nearly 20 years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-scale distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby), comfortably exceeding the 15-year requirement
Current, practical AI fluency: builds AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow frameworks at re:cinq, directly matching the cloud/AI builder-and-code preferred qualification

Proven entrepreneurial and community leadership: founded and still runs GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map), plus conference talks and multi-day workshops that demonstrate the ability to inspire and share insights
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, a stated preferred qualification
Prior titles are Senior/Staff engineer; no formal Principal- or Director-level strategy role on record
AI-specific work is recent (2025 onward) and tooling-focused, without a widely recognized AI product or platform attributed to him yet

key_factors.factor: Depth and breadth of engineering experience (~20 years, full stack to low-latency infrastructure)
Hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work with cloud/Kubernetes fluency
Founder experience and public knowledge-sharing (talks, workshops, open source)

response.justification: James Smith presents a compelling profile for a builder-oriented Principal Engineer in AI strategy: he clears the 15-year experience bar with roughly two decades spanning embedded systems, top-10 consumer apps, high-throughput ad-tech infrastructure, and cloud-native product work, and his current role centers on exactly the frontier this position targets — AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generation, and agentic workflows. His decade-long founder role at GISCollective satisfies the entrepreneurial preference and shows he can identify opportunities, ship platforms, and sustain them with real users, while his workshops and conference talks evidence the outward-facing, inspire-and-advise dimension of the role. The gaps — no AI graduate degree and no prior Principal title — are credential gaps rather than capability gaps, offset by demonstrated code-and-design fluency across the full stack and active work at the cutting edge of AI developer tooling.
